level_signals: Compensation range $441K-$490K consistent with senior IC at OpenAI
Expected to own high-impact product areas from strategy through execution
'Have significant product management experience' signals seniority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have significant product management experience, ideally in healthcare, enterprise software, AI, or other complex regulated environments.
Are excited about zero-to-one product development and know how to turn ambiguity into momentum.
Can operate equally well at the strategy level and in day-to-day execution details.
Build strong cross-functional partnerships and communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Have strong judgment about trust, safety, privacy, and quality in high-stakes user workflows.
Care deeply about improving healthcare through technology and are motivated by mission-driven product work.

scope.surface: ChatGPT Healthcare — products for clinicians, care teams, and healthcare organizations
primary_metrics_inferred: Product adoption by clinicians and health systems
Business impact / revenue from healthcare segment
core_responsibilities: Define product strategy and roadmap for key ChatGPT Healthcare experiences and capabilities
Identify the highest-impact problems across clinicians, healthcare operators, and health systems, and turn them into clear product priorities
Partner closely with engineering, research, design, legal, policy, and GTM teams to ship products that are useful, safe, and scalable
Drive execution from early discovery and prototype through launch, adoption, and iteration
Translate customer needs, technical constraints, and regulatory considerations into strong product decisions and crisp requirements
Use data, customer feedback, and market insight to improve product quality, trust, and business impact

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($441K-$490K)
Clear product surface with meaningful mission (healthcare AI)
High-impact, zero-to-one product ownership at a leading AI company
Cross-functional scope is well-defined (engineering, research, design, legal, policy, GTM)
red_flags: Role is onsite in San Francisco — no remote or hybrid option stated, limiting geographic flexibility
No explicit years-of-experience requirement makes seniority bar ambiguous
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Regulatory and compliance complexity in healthcare may slow shipping pace
Compensation range may represent total cash or include equity — not explicitly clarified

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from compensation range and ownership expectations; not stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description targeting health systems and clinicians)
location.type (stated as 'based in San Francisco, CA'; treated as onsite since no hybrid/remote mentioned)
compensation total_comp (unclear whether range is base only or total cash; left as base_range_usd)
ambiguities: Years of experience not quantified — 'significant product management experience' is subjective; severity treated as hard given unconditional bullet placement under 'You might thrive if you' which doubles as requirements language
Compensation range ($441K-$490K) not explicitly labeled as base salary vs. total cash; treated as base_range_usd given no equity or bonus mention
Visa sponsorship not addressed anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'

The 'You might thrive in this role if you' section is used as the requirements section — items treated as hard must-haves given no separate 'Preferred'/'Nice to have' section exists and all bullets are unconditional
